name: Artificial Algae Algorithm
subtitle: A bio-inspired metaheuristic algorithm for nonlinear global optimization
summary: A bio-inspired population-based metaheuristic optimization algorithm inspired by selected living behaviours of microalgae.

publication.title: Artificial algae algorithm (AAA) for nonlinear global optimization
publication.authors: Sait Ali Uymaz, Gulay Tezel, Esra Yel

motivation: AAA was proposed for nonlinear global optimization, where objective landscapes may be complex, multimodal, or constrained.
mechanism: Artificial algae colonies represent candidate solutions. Helical movement, evolutionary change, and adaptation combine to balance exploration and exploitation through fitness-based behaviour.

components.title: Helical Movement
components.description: Colonies move toward a light source through energy-dependent helical movement. Shear force and friction surface influence the movement, supporting a balance between exploration and exploitation.

components.title: Evolutionary Process
components.description: Colony growth is related to fitness and nutrient availability, with inspiration from the Monod model. Larger colonies represent better solutions, and the smallest colony may receive replicated cells from the largest colony.

components.title: Adaptation Process
components.description: Starvation-based adaptation allows insufficiently grown colonies to try to resemble the largest colony. An adaptation parameter controls this process.

benchmarking: Comparison with ABC, BA, DE, ACOR, and HSPOP
Pressure vessel design optimization compared with ABC and SPSO2011
